## Jordan Groshans Investment Analysis - October 2020

**Status at Purchase Date (2020-10-13):**
Groshans was a surprise 12th overall 2018 draft pick who dominated Low-A in 2019 before a left foot injury ended his season after 23 games. He spent 2020 at the Blue Jays' alternate training site and led the team in home runs, giving investors reason for optimism. At the time, the 21-year-old had considerable prospect credibility, though investment in his cards was betting on future performance from a prospect with significant injury history.

**Card Values & Market:**
While specific October 2020 card prices are difficult to pinpoint, his 2020 Bowman Chrome prospect cards (BP-104) were actively circulated in the hobby at that time, and base versions are currently valued at modest levels—suggesting limited appreciation from that period.

**Why the Investment Failed:**
The core problem: the power that scouts raved over seems to have dried up, with Groshans managing just one barrel out of 48 batted balls in 2022. He missed playing time in May 2021 due to a sore back and was placed on the injured list in early September; over 75 games he slashed .291/.367/.450—solid contact rates but lacking impact.
